rationale: Both correctly capture the two core resolved points (attribution already covered by #3576's {stage,replica} labels; tail_waste/fragmentation dropped per LHXuuu's pushback), but X omits the explicit scope negotiation (LLM_AR/Generation first, diffusion deferred to Q3) that was a real multi-comment thread thread, while Y misses it entirely — a completeness gap for Y. X's claim that the make_stats throttle is 'per-scheduler, not global' backed by an oddly specific 'code comment that explicitly guards against a second global throttle' reads as fabricated/unverifiable and is asserted with unwarranted confidence, whereas Y's parallel speculation on the same throttle is more hedged ('most plausibly,' 'worth a closer look') and appropriately escalates to filing a separate issue rather than asserting resolution. Y also grounds its claims across more files/line ranges (stat_logger.py, definitions.py, both omni and upstream vllm), giving richer if still partly unverifiable evidence. Y overstates finality by declaring the issue 'closing' when the actual thread just tapers off without an explicit close comment, a minor overreach against X's more conservative 'keep-open.'
